evals.prompt: Create a block plugin module called `content_recommendations` for a Drupal 10 site. The block should display recommended content items. It needs to inject a service for fetching recommendations via dependency injection. The block should have a configuration option for how many items to show (default: 5). Include proper block configuration form and default configuration. We want D11 compatibility.
evals.expected_output: Block with ContainerFactoryPluginInterface, 4-param create(), parent::__construct with 3 plugin params, blockForm/blockSubmit
expectations: Block plugin create() method has exactly 4 parameters: $container, $configuration, $plugin_id, $plugin_definition (NOT 1 parameter like controller create() -- plugin manager passes 4 args, using controller signature causes fatal TypeError)

Constructor calls parent::__construct($configuration, $plugin_id, $plugin_definition) with the 3 plugin params BEFORE storing injected services (missing parent call breaks block configuration, default values, and plugin inspection)
Block uses blockForm() for configuration form (NOT buildForm() which is the standalone form method -- blockForm receives pre-built form from block admin UI)
Block uses blockSubmit() for saving config to $this->configuration (NOT submitForm(), NOT Config API -- block config is managed by the block placement system, not the Config API)
defaultConfiguration() method exists and returns an array with config defaults including the item count setting

Block plugin uses EITHER D11 #[Block(...)] attribute with new TranslatableMarkup() OR D10 @Block annotation with @Translation() -- but NOT @Translation() inside a PHP attribute (mixing annotation helpers in attributes causes fatal error)
The module enables successfully: ddev drush en content_recommendations returns exit code 0
Generated module code passes phpcs --standard=Drupal,DrupalPractice static analysis without errors
